beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, light breakfast, check phone.
beats.reasoning: Amara is an early riser; 6:30am fits her natural rhythm. Full energy on day one, she starts with her morning routine and coffee before heading out.

beats.activity: Walk to neighborhood coffee shop, shoot a few frames with film camera.
beats.reasoning: Sunny Monday morning is perfect for a photographic walk; she takes her film camera and enjoys the weather before the workday, stopping for a second coffee and a moment of calm.

beats.activity: Commute to South Lake Union practice.
beats.reasoning: Drive or bus to her pediatric dentist practice in SLU; Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm but she's arriving well before that window, so no impact on her commute.

beats.activity: See pediatric dental patients, manage practice.
beats.reasoning: Full morning of patient appointments; she intentionally schedules her day to wrap by noon so she can catch the 11am FIFA match (USA vs Wales) at a nearby bar or return home if not interested—but given the sunny weather and social energy, she may duck out early for lunch near the match.

beats.activity: Lunch and catch part of the FIFA match at a downtown bar.
beats.location_name: Belltown / Pike Place Market area, Seattle
beats.reasoning: The USA vs Wales match airs at 11am; Amara finishes work at noon and grabs lunch near downtown. She's not a huge soccer fan, but the sunny day and social atmosphere draw her in for a quick meal and a few minutes of the match before heading out.

beats.activity: Walk through Pike Place Market, photograph vendors and produce.
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny Monday for a leisurely stroll with her film camera. She captures the market light and energy, picks up fresh ingredients for evening sourdough or a meal prep.

beats.activity: Errand: pop into Whole Foods for sourdough starter supplies and fresh produce.
beats.reasoning: She's downtown anyway; grabs what she needs for baking and dinner prep. Light Rail closure doesn't affect her since she's walking/driving in the downtown core.

beats.activity: Commute back home to Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Drive or walk back home; Light Rail is still closed (9am–3pm maintenance), but she avoids it and uses surface transit or car. She has the afternoon free to relax.

beats.activity: Knitting project, organize sourdough starter, prep dinner ingredients.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon downtime: she settles into knitting, tends to her sourdough starter, and gets ingredients ready. The sunny weather makes her want to stay in and be creative. Self-reliant and content with solo time.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner: fresh vegetables, sourdough bread, light protein.
beats.reasoning: Cooks a simple, wholesome dinner at home using ingredients from Pike Place and Whole Foods. She eats mindfully, enjoying the day's light through the window.

beats.activity: Review film photos from the day, journal, light knitting, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down: she develops notes on her film shots from the morning and market, does some light knitting, and gets ready for sleep. Early riser prepares for an early night.
Full night's sleep (8h 15m), consistent with her early-riser schedule. Ends just after 6am, ready for another early morning.

diary:
Started the day early with coffee on Capitol Hill—the weather was too perfect to waste. Grabbed my film camera and wandered over to Molly Moon's, catching the morning light on the storefronts. The commute to the practice was smooth, and I had a full slate of patients this morning; there's something grounding about working with kids' teeth, their nervous energy, their trust.

Around eleven, I ducked out to catch some of the USA-Wales match at a bar in Belltown. The place was already buzzing even though it was early. After, I meandered through Pike Place Market with my camera again—the vendors were vibrant today, and I shot a whole roll of their hands, produce, expressions. Picked up sourdough starter supplies at Whole Foods on the way home.

Back at the house, I fed the starter, worked on my knitting, prepped dinner. The evening felt unhurried. Developed some film prints mentally while journaling. There's a rhythm to days like this—purposeful but not rushed.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive to South Lake Union anyway, but it made me grateful for flexibility. The FIFA match pulled me downtown; I don't follow soccer closely, but the energy was contagious, and I needed a break from the clinical morning. Grabbing my camera at Pike Place felt natural—I've been wanting to build a visual diary of the neighborhood vendors. The sourdough starter project has been calling to me for weeks, so today felt like permission to finally begin. My mood shifted from task-oriented (patients, appointments) to exploratory (photography, market wandering) to contemplative (home, creativity). That arc felt earned and necessary.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
